pattern: Once the scores are sorted, the tightest possible spread for any group of k of them always comes from k values that are consecutive in that sorted order, so only those windows need checking.
hints: Picking k values with the smallest possible gap between their max and min is easier once the scores are sorted -- then you're really choosing a contiguous stretch of the sorted list.
For a fixed window length k, the spread of a contiguous window is just its last element minus its first, so slide a window of size k across the sorted array.
Special-case k == 1: any single score has a spread of zero on its own. Otherwise, the answer is the minimum of (sorted[i + k - 1] - sorted[i]) over every valid starting index i.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, four scores choosing two
k is one regardless of spread among the scores
all identical scores give a zero spread window
k close to the array length forces a specific window
scores already spread across the full value range
single score, k equal to one

stress.description: random scores up to the length cap with a random window size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
